chief_complaint: A 65-year-old woman presented with sudden onset of chest pain and shortness of breath.
history: She had been on pembrolizumab for metastatic melanoma for 6 months. Two weeks prior to admission, she developed a dry cough and worsening shortness of breath. She denied fever, chills, or sputum production. She had a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. She was a non-smoker and had no history of lung disease.
exam: Vital signs: Temperature 36.5°C, Blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, Pulse 110 bpm, Respiratory rate 24/min. Physical examination revealed a diffuse crackles in the bilateral lung fields.
labs: ECG: ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, and aVF. Blood tests: Elevated troponin (0.5 ng/mL), normal white blood cell count. Chest CT: Bilateral ground-glass opacities and interstitial markings. Pulmonary function tests: Normal.
correct_diagnosis: Takotsubo Syndrome triggered by Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor-induced Pneumonitis
differentials: Takotsubo Syndrome triggered by Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor-induced Pneumonitis
correct_ranking: Takotsubo Syndrome triggered by Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor-induced Pneumonitis
